The plan includes first-stage fine-tuning: Fine-tune ViT-B/32 CLIP on COCO (ShareGPT4V) using CyCLIP loss (L_CLIP + 0.25 L_I-Cyclic + 0.25 L_C-Cyclic) with AdamW (lr 1e-6, weight decay 1e-2), batch size 128, temperature log scale 4.6052, for 3 epochs. Yields base model (Long-CyCLIP-only + MCSIE, LCCOM).
The plan includes extract normalized embeddings (L2-normalized) from frozen image and text encoders for a set of samples; compute modality-specific mean vectors (image mean  ar{x}, text mean  ar{y}).

The plan includes i0T_post standardization: For each normalized embedding, subtract the corresponding modality mean and re-normalize by Frobenius norm: x_i' = Normalize(x_i -  ar{x}), y_i' = Normalize(y_i -  ar{y}).

The plan includes i0T_async setup: After first-stage fine-tuning, freeze encoders and add independent batch normalization layers (BN_img, BN_txt) with learnable weights W and biases b after the normalized embedding outputs.

The plan includes mCSIE augmentation: Apply dropout (rate 0.1) to every multi-head attention layer in the frozen image encoder (ViT) and text encoder (Transformer) to create augmented views of image and text embeddings.

The plan includes train BN layers asynchronously using MCSIE loss: contrastive learning over all pairs of (original/ augmented) image and text embeddings, using same optimizer settings as first stage, with frozen encoders.

The plan includes during BN training, update running means of normalized embeddings per modality with averaging factor alpha=0.1:  ar{x}_{t+1} = alpha *  ar{x}_B + (1-alpha) *  ar{x}_t, similarly for text.
The plan includes after BN training, acquire final train statistics:  ar{x}_train,  ar{y}_train and variances sigma_x, sigma_y (observed close to 1.0).

The plan includes i0T_async inference: For each normalized embedding, subtract train mean, divide by sqrt(variance + epsilon=1e-5), apply BN linear transform (W, b), then re-normalize by Frobenius norm.

The plan includes compute modality gap metrics: Centroid Distance (CD) as Frobenius norm between mean normalized image and text embeddings; Linear Separability (LS) as 1 - MSE of linear regression classifying image vs. text embeddings; assign Severity Level (low if CD<0.19, moderate if 0.19<=CD<0.63, severe if CD>=0.63).

The plan includes evaluate downstream tasks: image-text retrieval (I2T, T2I Recall@1 on Flickr30k), zero-shot classification (top-1 accuracy on CIFAR100 and CUB-200), and relative correlation with human judgments (Flickr8k-Expert, Flickr8k-CF).

The plan includes the setting 'I0T_post': Post-hoc embedding standardization: subtract mean vector of normalized embeddings per modality and re-normalize by Frobenius norm on frozen encoders from first-stage fine-tuning.

The plan includes the setting 'I0T_async': Trainable method adding independent batch normalization layers for image and text encoders, trained asynchronously after first stage with MCSIE augmentation to learn modality-specific means/variances.
The plan includes the use of the COCO (ShareGPT4V) dataset.
The plan includes the use of the Flickr30k dataset.
The plan includes the use of the CIFAR100 dataset.
The plan includes the use of the CUB-200 (Birds) dataset.
The plan includes the use of the Flickr8k-Expert dataset.
The plan includes the use of the Flickr8k-CF dataset.
The plan includes the use of the ViT-B/32-based Long-CLIP model.
The plan includes the use of the BLIP model.

The plan includes the MG_{λ=0.5} baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the MG_{λ=-0.5} baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the CLOOB baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the Unif-Align baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the PAC-S baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the BLIP baseline for comparison.
The plan includes the Centroid Distance (CD) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Linear Separability (LS) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Severity Level evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Image-to-Text Retrieval (I2T) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Text-to-Image Retrieval (T2I) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Classification Accuracy (CIFAR100) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Classification Accuracy (Birds) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Relative Correlation (Expert) evaluation metric.
The plan includes the Relative Correlation (CF) evaluation metric.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'learning_rate' with value 1e-6.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'weight_decay' with value 1e-2.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'batch_size' with value 128.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'temperature_log_scale' with value 4.6052.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'optimizer' with value AdamW.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'alpha (averaging factor for BN running means)' with value 0.1.

source.ref: alpha (averaging factor for BN running means)

description: The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'dropout_rate (for MCSIE augmentation)' with value 0.1.
The plan specifies the hyperparameter 'eps' with value 1e-5.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 1: Demonstrates the development process leading to I0T_async, showing that asynchronous BN training (+BN*) reduces modality gap to moderate level while maintaining downstream performance.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 2: I0T_post reduces modality gap to near-zero (CD 0.0102, LS 0.5374) and I0T_async to moderate (CD 0.4795, LS 0.9960), both without hurting overall downstream tasks; I0T_post achieves best retrieval scores.

The execution outcomes match the results in Table 3: Using as few as 32 test samples for I0T_post already reduces modality gap to low severity (CD 0.1880) and achieves competitive retrieval, with performance saturating as more samples are used.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 3: I0T_post effectively removes peak activations and modality-specific characteristics, reducing CD to near zero, outperforming clipping and Mind-the-Gap in suppressing modality-specific patterns.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 4: I0T methods achieve much lower centroid distance than BLIP with fewer parameters, while maintaining competitive correlation scores on Flickr8k-Expert.

The execution outcomes match the results in Figure 5: I0T-S (I0T_post) yields a wider cosine similarity distribution (range -0.349 to 0.660) with mean near zero, enabling more intuitive and explainable scoring compared to CLIP-S and PAC-S.
The execution outcomes match the results in Table 8: Ablation shows that MCSIE loss further reduces modality gap during I0T_async training compared to training without it.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 2): I0T_post achieves low modality gap (CD 0.0102, LS 0.5374) while achieving T2I retrieval recall 76.30% on Flickr30k, outperforming CLIP's 67.10%.
The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 2): I0T_async reduces modality gap to moderate level (CD 0.4795, LS 0.9960) and improves T2I retrieval to 73.80% over CLIP.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Figure 3): Post-hoc embedding standardization (I0T_post) eliminates peak activations and modality-specific characteristics, lowering CD to near zero, unlike clipping or Mind-the-Gap.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Figure 5): I0TScore (I0T_post) yields a non-skewed cosine similarity distribution with range [-0.349, 0.660] and mean near zero, enabling more intuitive scoring than CLIP-S and PAC-S.

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 3): Using only 32 test samples with I0T_post reduces CD to low severity (<0.19) and achieves competitive retrieval (I2T 70.30%, T2I 73.00%).

The execution outcomes match the specific claim (based on Table 8): The asynchronous BN training with MCSIE loss (I0T_async) further reduces modality gap compared to standard training, as shown in ablation.
